evidence: Dashboard data now exposes active_count, archived_count, per-run run_state, data_quality_warnings, and next_work candidate arrays.
GitHub open issues are collected as best-effort candidates and inbox candidates are represented even when empty.
deductions: The dashboard UI still keeps some pre-existing mojibake text, so the visual output is not fully polished.

evidence: Changes are limited to harness dashboard generation, dashboard rendering, validation script, and issue #47 artifacts.
The generator keeps local dashboard generation working when GitHub collection fails.
A focused validation script checks the new dashboard data contract.
deductions: Inbox candidate parsing is intentionally simple and may need a structured parser if inbox entries become more complex.
Dashboard rendering still uses the existing inline JavaScript structure rather than a more testable shared renderer.

evidence: Ran syntax checks, dashboard data generation, dashboard data validation, status diagnosis, data shape inspection, and local static HTTP response check.
deductions: Playwright accessibility snapshot could not be captured because the browser session closed during navigation.
No automated browser assertion was added for the rendered dashboard DOM.

evidence: GitHub collection failure is captured as a warning instead of failing dashboard generation.
No product code or GitHub issue mutation was included.
The initially missing user decision was corrected, and the final spec reflects the explicit choice to include GitHub open issues.
Plan separated the unresolved next-work candidate decision from implementation and then proceeded after confirmation.
Used issue #47, existing dashboard generator, history data, diagnose-status output, and prior priority labels.
deductions: The implementation did not fully normalize existing archived history text that was already mojibake.

evidence: Task spec, plan, spec, implementation notes, verification, and review artifacts are present.
deductions: The work initially wrote a spec before the user decision and had to be corrected.
Rendered dashboard verification is documented as partial because Playwright snapshot failed.
The handoff still leaves known UI text cleanup as residual work.

strengths: Dashboard data now clearly distinguishes active and archived runs.
Next work candidates include GitHub open issues and inbox candidates as requested.
A reusable dashboard data validator protects the new data contract.
weaknesses: Some existing dashboard copy remains mojibake.
The process required correction after an initially premature spec decision.
recommended_next_action: PASS. Create a PR after optionally deciding whether to handle the remaining dashboard copy encoding cleanup as a separate issue.
